description.task: Write a Java program that classifies and demonstrates the differences between unit tests and integration tests. Build a test catalog system that organizes tests by type and scope.
instructions: Implement groupByType() using stream().collect(Collectors.groupingBy()) or a manual loop with LinkedHashMap.
Implement getTestPyramidHealth() by counting unit tests and dividing by total tests * 100.
Output shows grouped tests and pyramid health percentage.
description.hint: The Test Pyramid: many unit tests (fast, isolated), fewer integration tests (slower, requires infrastructure), fewest E2E tests (slowest, full system). Spring Boot Test supports all three layers.
description.expectedOutput:
UNIT tests (4): 
  [UNIT] calculateTax_positiveAmount (scope: TaxService, external: false)
  [UNIT] calculateTax_zeroAmount (scope: TaxService, external: false)
  [UNIT] validateEmail_validFormat (scope: Validator, external: false)
  [UNIT] validateEmail_invalidFormat (scope: Validator, external: false)
INTEGRATION tests (2): 
  [INTEGRATION] saveUser_persistsToDatabase (scope: UserRepository, external: true)
  [INTEGRATION] createOrder_updatesInventory (scope: OrderService, external: true)
END_TO_END tests (1): 
  [END_TO_END] checkoutFlow_completePurchase (scope: Full Stack, external: true)
Unit test percentage: 57.1% (Needs more unit tests)

title: Write Unit Tests for a Calculator

description.task: Implement a Calculator class and write comprehensive unit tests for it. The tests should cover happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions.
instructions: In runAll(), write at least 8 assertions covering all 6 Calculator methods.
Include at least 2 exception tests (divide by zero, negative square root).
Include edge cases: multiply by 0, add negative numbers, power of 0.
All tests should PASS — the Calculator implementation is correct.
description.hint: In JUnit 5: @Test void addPositiveNumbers() { assertEquals(5.0, calc.add(2.0, 3.0), 0.001); }. Use assertThrows() for exception tests. Each test method tests ONE thing.
description.expectedOutput:
PASS: add positive numbers
PASS: add negative numbers
PASS: subtract
PASS: multiply
PASS: multiply by zero
PASS: divide
PASS: divide by zero throws exception
PASS: power of 2
PASS: square root
PASS: square root of negative throws exception

Test Results: 10 passed, 0 failed

description.task: Implement a simple test coverage tracker that records which methods were invoked during tests and calculates line/method coverage percentages.
description.context: Code coverage tools like JaCoCo measure how much of your code is exercised by tests. Spring Boot projects typically target 80%+ coverage. Maven/Gradle integrate with JaCoCo automatically.
instructions: Implement getUncoveredMethods() as allMethods stream filtered to not be in coveredMethods.
First report shows partial coverage; second shows full coverage.
description.hint: JaCoCo generates coverage reports during Maven build (mvn test). Add the jacoco-maven-plugin to pom.xml. Spring Boot Test with @SpringBootTest typically covers integration paths.
description.expectedOutput:
Method Coverage: 60.0% (3/5 methods)
Uncovered methods:
  - UserService.updateUser
  - UserService.deleteUser

After adding more tests:
Method Coverage: 100.0% (5/5 methods)
Full coverage achieved!
